nodes.name: actuator modules
nodes.logic: Humanoid robots require dozens of precise, compact, and high-torque actuators for each joint. Actuator manufacturers will see demand surge as production scales from prototypes to thousands of units. Revenue is levered to robot unit volumes and actuator content per robot, which grows as joints become more numerous.

nodes.name: high-density battery packs
nodes.logic: Untethered humanoid operation demands high energy density and fast charging. Battery pack suppliers benefit from increased orders for robotic power systems. Revenue growth is tied to both robot adoption and battery pack size, which is substantial for humanoid payloads.

nodes.name: depth cameras and LiDAR
nodes.logic: Humanoid robots need robust perception for navigation and manipulation. Suppliers of 3D vision sensors will see increased procurement from robot manufacturers. Sensor volume per robot is high, and as robot production scales, sensor revenue grows disproportionately.

nodes.name: robot simulation software
nodes.logic: Before deployment, humanoid robots must be trained and tested in virtual environments. Simulation software providers license their platforms to robot developers and end-users for training reinforcement learning models. Sales are tied to the number of robot variants and customers, not just robot units, providing leverage.

nodes.name: custom assembly systems
nodes.logic: Humanoid robot production requires unique assembly lines for complex mechanical and electronic integration. Custom automation equipment makers will receive contracts from robot OEMs to build factories. Revenue is upfront and lumpy but directly proportional to capital expenditure on robot manufacturing capacity.

nodes.name: warehouse automation
nodes.logic: Humanoid robots can perform picking, sorting, and packing tasks in warehouses. Logistics companies that adopt these robots will see labor cost reductions and throughput improvements. Their margins expand as they replace variable labor with fixed robot costs, increasing profitability.

nodes.name: flexible manufacturing
nodes.logic: Automotive plants use many fixed automation systems. Humanoid robots offer flexibility to switch between tasks and vehicle models. Automakers adopting them will reduce retooling costs and improve line utilization, boosting return on assets.

nodes.name: temporary labor providers
nodes.logic: As humanoid robots replace manual tasks in warehousing and manufacturing, demand for temporary low-skill workers declines. Staffing agencies face revenue erosion and lower margins. The effect intensifies as robot costs fall and capabilities expand.
